era_context: Late Archaic Greece; Lydia under Alyattes II; Neo-Babylonian Empire under Nabopolassar in its early phase; the Spring and Autumn period in China with interstate competition; the Levant experiencing transitions in power and influence.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 631 BCE is essentially non-existent. No contemporary sources record specific events for this year. Ancient chronographers and later historians do not provide reliable year-by-year accounts for this period. The year falls within the reigns of major figures (Alyattes II, Nabopolassar) whose broader activities are known, but no specific events are attested for 631 BCE. This year exemplifies the fundamental limitation of ancient history: most years leave no historical record whatsoever.
